fixItComments.body: Agent follow-up on June 10, 2026: I rechecked production against this request. The current dashboard-to-objectives flow already supports the core filter stack you described: clicking the 7 days due-horizon bucket sends me to /?page=objectives&due=7&scope=company&active=1, and that Objectives view keeps the due-date lens active while still allowing additional status filters like On Track, At Risk, Blocked, and Not Started. The remaining open question is product structure, not a broken workflow: do you want the top-level Dashboard summary buckets themselves to deep-link into that same centralized list pattern more aggressively, or do you want a broader information-architecture merge between Dashboard and Objectives? I am leaving this in progress until Andrew/Jake/Tim confirm that desired structure.

Agent verification on June 10, 2026: I re-ran the live Dashboard -> Objectives flow in production. Dashboard Active opens /?page=objectives&active=1, and Dashboard 7 days opens /?page=objectives&due=7&scope=company&active=1. In both cases the Objectives view keeps the lens active and still exposes the status filters (All, On Track, At Risk, Blocked, Not Started, Completed). I do not have a fresh broken workflow to patch here; the remaining ask is a product-direction decision about whether Dashboard should become a more aggressively centralized Objective List entry point.

activeFixIt.body:
Within the main dashboard, there is some navigation language and workflow logic that is causing confusion.

The objective task categories currently displayed on the main dashboard are:

Open
Overdue
Due Today
Due Within 7 Days
Due Within 14 Days
Due Within 28 Days
Completed

These are good and should be considered the core visuals for team productivity.

However, when a user clicks into Open, for example, the next screen only shows the following status options:

All
On Track
At Risk
Blocked
Not Started
Completed

These should be treated as sub-filters under the main dashboard categories, not separate navigation areas.

For example, on the main dashboard, Open should show a breakdown of how many open objectives are:

On Track
At Risk
Blocked
Not Started

Then, when a user clicks Open, it should only take them to the open objectives. From there, the user should be able to apply additional filters without needing to navigate back to the home screen to view another category such as Due Within 7 Days.

Would it work better to have one Objective List view with filter selections instead of forcing users to navigate through multiple areas of the platform?

The suggested filter structure would be:

First filter: Ownership / Scope
My Objectives
Individual Person
Group / Department
All Objectives

Second filter: Main Category
All
Open
Overdue
Due Today
Due Within 7 Days
Due Within 14 Days
Due Within 28 Days
Completed

Third filter: Status
All
On Track
At Risk
Blocked
Not Started
Completed

The goal is to make the objective view more inclusive and flexible so users can quickly see what they need without moving through multiple screens. For example, a user should be able to select:

My Objectives + Open + At Risk
Operations Group + Due Within 7 Days + Blocked
All Objectives + Overdue + Not Started

This would create one centralized view where the user can filter by ownership, due-date category, and status. It should reduce confusion, improve visibility, and make it easier for teams and managers to identify what needs attention.
